q1_sink_dangerous.evidence: Sink is eval(expr, {"__builtins__": {}}, {"amount": 42, "fee": 7}) on line 87, which executes arbitrary Python expressions from user input, constituting code injection / RCE.

mock_guidance.target: Mock the built-in eval function or wrap the eval call on line 87 to intercept execution.
mock_guidance.capture: Capture the exact string value of the expr argument passed to eval to verify it matches user input from request.GET.get("expr").
mock_guidance.fake_behavior: Return a safe default value such as 49 (the result of the default expression "amount + fee") or a fixed numeric value, allowing the function to proceed to the JsonResponse without executing the tainted expression.
notes: Even with __builtins__ restricted, eval can still execute dangerous expressions, so the call must not actually execute in tests.
If the test framework supports it, assert that eval is called and capture its arguments without invoking the real eval.
